# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- About Us
- Programmes
- Ph.D Students

Department of Computer Science and Engineering is from one of the very first departments established at IIT Jammu with a vision to foster teaching, research and development of technologies in areas of computing and information technology. The department has a good mix of young and experienced faculties in the domain of Cyber and Information Security, AI and Machine Learning, Software Engineering to name a few. The department runs a regular B.Tech programme and M.Tech programmes in Information Security and Data Science. Some of the focus areas of the department includes Post Quantum Cryptography, Future Internet Protocols and Security Developments, Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Computer Vision and Machine Learning. The research scholars in the department are also working in a variety of challenging problems.

text: This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Aroof Aimen. The group focuses on various AI-related research areas, including failure cases of Vision Language models, multi-task learning, few-shot learning, generative models, and applying AI for healthcare purposes. They are connected through a relationship of "RESEARCHES_IN" with 5 other entities, indicating collaboration or association in these research areas.

The Community 1 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Gaurav Varshney. This group consists of four PhD students (Chandranshu Gupta, Jaswinder Kour, Mahendra Kumar Gurve, and Rina Mishra) who are supervised by Dr. Varshney. The community focuses on various research areas related to cybersecurity, including anti-phishing, internet security protocols, email and web security, vulnerability assessment, threat modeling, network security, and the application of machine learning in cyber security.

The Community 2 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Harkeerat Kaur. The group consists of PhD students Palak Verma and Yogita Chhabra, who work under the supervision of Dr. Kaur. Their research focuses on Computer Vision, Image Processing, Biometrics, Visual Cryptography, Blockchains, Artificial Intelligence, and Data Privacy, with a specific emphasis on the intersection of Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision.

The Community 3 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Manoj Singh Gaur and Shaifu Gupta. They oversee the research of PhD students Pragti Jamwal and Rishika Kohli, who are working on various aspects of cyber security, including networks on chip, malware analysis, and simulation of streaming protocols. The group's research focuses on areas such as computer networks, operating systems, VLSI design, cloud computing, machine learning, and federated learning, with a specific emphasis on security issues in these domains.

This community in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Mrinmoy Bhattacharjee, Sarada Prasad Gochhayat, Sayantan Mukherjee, and Sumit Kumar Pandey. The group focuses on research areas including audio and speech processing, machine/deep learning, natural language processing, and cryptography, with a specific emphasis on security and privacy in distributed networks and systems. One PhD student, Abhilash Kumar Das, is associated with this community under the supervision of one of these faculty members.

The Community 5 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Samaresh Bera, a faculty member with expertise in resource allocation, network slicing, security, and IoT/IIoT in 5G/6G networks. Aqsa Sayeed, a PhD student under Dr. Bera's supervision, is likely working on related research topics. This group focuses on advancing the capabilities of 5G and emerging 6G networks through innovative solutions in communication and network security.

The Community 6 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is a research-focused group led by Faculty member Yamuna Prasad. The group consists of PhD students Anuj, Pardeep Kaur, Sankar Behera, and Yukti Gupta, who are supervised by Yamuna Prasad. They conduct research in areas including Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Big Data, Soft Computing, and Cyber-Physical System Security, with specific application domains in Natural Language Processing, Bioinformatics, and NeuroSymbolic AI.

The Community 7 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty Member Sidharth Maheshwari. The group focuses on innovative research areas including Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, and Machine Learning (specifically Tsetlin Machines). Their work involves developing energy-efficient genome analysis solutions and improving battery management systems in electric vehicles through the integration of machine learning at the edge.

The Community 8 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Subhasis Bhattacharjee. The group consists of PhD students Malti Baghel, Pintu Naik, and Surendra Tyagi, who are supervised by Bhattacharjee. Their research focuses on various areas including Distributed Algorithms, Ad Hoc and Sensor Networks, VLSI Testing, and Security in Networks and IOT.

The Community 9 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Suman Banerjee. The group consists of four PhD students: Alex Joseph, Dildar Ali, Pooja, and Poonam Sharma, who are supervised by Dr. Banerjee. This community focuses on research areas including social network analysis, algorithmic data management, graph theory, and security through differential privacy, among others.

The Community 10 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Vinit Jakhetiya. The group consists of PhD students Ajeet Kumar Verma, Ambreen Bashir, Deebha Mumtaz, Rishabh Shukla, Sadbhawna, and Zeenat Fatima, who are supervised by Dr. Jakhetiya. This community focuses on research areas including Visual Quality Assessment, Perceptual Signal Modeling, Computer Vision, and Image Processing, among others, with applications in Video and Medical Processing, 3D Reconstruction, and the application of Machine/Deep Learning techniques.
